findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java line 83 — file type validation relies solely on the client-provided Content-Type header (file.getContentType()), which an attacker can set to any allowed value while uploading a file of any dangerous type. No magic-byte / server-side content inspection is performed.

findings.name: Unchecked Input for Loop Condition
findings.description: The product does not properly check inputs that are used for loop conditions, potentially leading to a denial of service or other consequences because of excessive looping.
findings.evidence: HomeController.java:68 — for (MultipartFile file : files) iterates over a user-supplied list (@RequestParam("files") List<MultipartFile> files) with no upper-bound check on the number of files. Only a null/empty guard exists, so an attacker can submit arbitrarily many files and cause excessive looping / resource exhaustion.

findings.name: Improper Initialization
findings.description: The product does not initialize or incorrectly initializes a resource, which might leave the resource in an unexpected state when it is accessed or used.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:115 — byte[] photoData = null; is initialized to null. If file.getBytes() throws a non-IOException RuntimeException, it is silently swallowed by catch (Exception ex) with only a warning log, and execution continues with photoData still null.

findings.name: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: When a security-critical event occurs, the product either does not record the event or omits important details about the event when logging it.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java (~line 99): The empty-file validation branch (file.getSize() <= 0) sets an error result and returns with no log statement. Additionally, the application has no authentication layer, so there is zero logging of authentication/authorization events.

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence:
src/main/resources/application.properties (lines 11-12): spring.datasource.username=photoalbum / spring.datasource.password=photoalbum
src/main/resources/application-docker.properties (lines 3-4): same credentials hard-coded in the Docker profile
